message:
style(stdio): rustfmt — re-alphabetise PrintfDispatchKind re-export + wrap nested-alternative match

Pure rustfmt reflow across two files after the PrintfDispatchKind enum
landed in the previous commit:

- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/mod.rs: the `pub use printf::{...}`
  re-export block is re-sorted so the newly-added `PrintfDispatchKind`
  + `printf_dispatch_kind` identifiers settle into alphabetical
  position (`PrintfDispatchKind` now sits after `Precision` and
  before `Width`, `printf_dispatch_kind` between `parse_format_string`
  and... — the sorting follows rustfmt's case-insensitive identifier
  order).

- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs: same re-sort on the `use
  frankenlibc_core::stdio::{...}` import block (PrintfDispatchKind
  relocates alphabetically), and the `match printf_dispatch_kind(...)`
  arm in the `extract_va_args!` macro has its nested-alternative
  pattern reflowed from
      Some(PrintfDispatchKind::LiteralPercent | PrintfDispatchKind::ErrnoMessage)
      | None => {}
  to
      Some(
          PrintfDispatchKind::LiteralPercent | PrintfDispatchKind::ErrnoMessage,
      )
      | None => {}
  per rustfmt's line-length budget.

`cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-abi -p frankenlibc-core` now reports
no drift; no behaviour change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
